title: Sample Preparation for Mass Spectrometry Using ZipTip C18
author: Neilier Junior
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.be45jgy6
materials: Name 4 microtubes of 1.5 mL with the respective reagents: ACN 100% (I) TFA 0.1% (I) TFA 0.1% (II) ACN 100% (II) Add 1 mL of the respective solutions to the tubes above.
Calibrate the P-10 pipette to 10 μL.

steps.title: Name new 0
steps.instruction: Name new 0.2 mL microtubes with sample names followed by “zt”.

steps.title: Aliquot 50 μL of ACN 100% in each “zt” tube
steps.instruction: Aliquot 50 μL of ACN 100% in each “zt” tube.

steps.title: During the entire process, no air should enter through ZipTip
steps.instruction: During the entire process, no air should enter through ZipTip. Pipetting should be done slowly to maximize chemical interactions

steps.title: Cleaning and preparation
steps.instruction: Cleaning and preparation: ACN 100% (I) Pipette the acetonitrile from the ACN 100% (I) tube 10 times, discarding the contents on the kimwipes

steps.title: Fit the ZipTip with the pipette...
steps.instruction: Fit the ZipTip with the pipette already attached, so as not to pass air through the "chromatographic column". Hold down until you start the procedure with the first pipetting.

steps.title: Equilibrate
steps.instruction: Equilibrate: TFA 0.1% (I) Pipette the acetonitrile from the TFA 0.1% (I) tube 10 times, discarding the contents on the kimwipes

steps.title: Binding of pepitides (analyte)
steps.instruction: Binding of pepitides (analyte): sample tube Homogenize the sample with ZipTip 20 times

steps.title: Wash
steps.instruction: Wash: TFA 0.1% (II) Pipette the acetonitrile from the TFA 0.1% (II) tube 10 times, discarding the contents on the kimwipes

steps.title: Elution
steps.instruction: Elution: ACN 100% (II) Homogenize 20 times in the “zt” tube, already with 50 μL of ACN100%

steps.title: Shutdown
steps.instruction: Shutdown: ACN 100% (II) Pipette the acetonitrile from the ACN 100% (II) 10 times, discarding in the “zt” tube.
